intro: Miamin (meaning the right hand) was the name of two figures in post-exilic Judah. One was a priest who returned from Babylon with Zerubbabel (Nehemiah 12:5), and another was among the Israelites who had married foreign women and agreed to divorce them in accordance with Ezra's reform (Ezra 10:25). The name is a variant spelling of Miniamin. As a priestly name it suggests an honorific connection to the right hand — the position of favor and strength.
